summary: Ukrainian forces reported extensive UAV attacks across multiple regions, destroying several bridges on the Crimean approach and claiming air defense kills of 195 hostile drones. Russian forces announced the liberation of the settlement Roskoshnoye in the Donetsk People's Republic and established control over Okrimovka in Kharkiv region. Both sides continue high‑intensity operations with significant civilian casualties reported in May.
key_points: Ukrainian air defenses shot down or suppressed 195 hostile Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and other UAVs across north, south and east Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/64833).
Ukrainian strikes damaged four bridges at the Crimean entrance, including two over the North Crimean Canal and the road bridge between Perekop and Armyansk (Source: @UkraineNow/81836).
Russian Ministry of Defense announced the liberation of the settlement Roskoshnoye in the DPR (Source: @tass_agency/380222).
Russian forces reported establishing control over the settlement Okrimovka in Kharkiv region (Source: @tass_agency/380221).
Ukrainian forces reported a Shahed strike on the Kuibyshev oil refinery, halting its operations (Source: @eRadarrua/96161).
Multiple reactive Shahed attacks were reported over Poltava, Sumy and Konotop, causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81837; @mon1tor_ua/67555; @mon1tor_ua/67542).
UN officials warned May was the deadliest month for Ukrainian civilians since 2022, citing mass casualties in Sumy region (Source: @UkraineNow/81823).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian air defenses claimed 195 hostile UAVs destroyed, while Ukrainian strikes hit four bridges on the Crimean approach. Russian forces announced the liberation of Roskoshnoye and control over Okrimovka, indicating ongoing territorial contests. Civilian casualties remain high, with May identified as the deadliest month since 2022.

points: Ukrainian air defense units, including aviation, anti‑aircraft missile troops, electronic warfare units and mobile fire groups, reported shooting down or suppressing 195 hostile Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and other UAVs across northern, southern and eastern sectors, with additional rocket impacts on nine locations and debris falls on eight sites (Source: @kpszsu/64833).
Ukrainian forces struck the Kuibyshev oil refinery, causing it to suspend operations after a drone attack, highlighting the targeting of energy infrastructure (Source: @eRadarrua/96161).

Multiple reactive Shahed attacks were logged over Poltava region, including a reactive Shahed over the Podilskyi district and another over the Almaznyi area, causing damage to energy facilities (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67555; @mon1tor_ua/67564).

In Sumy region, Ukrainian forces reported a Shahed strike on Kirikivka and another on Konotop, with additional UAV activity near Antonivka towards Karnaukhivka, indicating continued pressure on northeastern fronts (Source: @eRadarrua/96177).

Ukrainian reports indicated that a Shahed hit a railway passenger bus on the Melitopol‑Velyka Belozerka route, injuring five people including a teenager, and causing power supply problems in the area (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
The Russian Ministry of Defense announced the liberation of the settlement Roskoshnoye in the Donetsk People's Republic, confirming a territorial gain (Source: @tass_agency/380222).
Russian forces reported establishing control over the settlement Okrimovka in Kharkiv region, expanding their foothold in the north‑eastern theater (Source: @tass_agency/380221).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bomb strikes on Donetsk region, indicating continued air offensive operations (Source: @kpszsu/64843).
Russian authorities expressed outrage over alleged desecration of the "Mother Armenia" memorial in Gyumri, Armenia, reflecting diplomatic tensions (Source: @tass_agency/380225).
Russia’s Lavrov conducted talks in Moscow with the Bahraini foreign minister, underscoring diplomatic outreach amid the conflict (Source: @tass_agency/380232).

Ukrainian reports detailed small‑arms battles in the Sumy district of Bachevsk, Ivolzhanske, Pysarevka and Novaya Sich, showing ongoing ground combat in the north‑eastern sector (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).

In the Kharkiv direction, the Army Group "North" reported offensive actions toward Kazachya Lopan and fighting in villages Ohrimovka and Losevka in the Vovchansk sector (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Ukrainian forces claimed to have struck the Afipsky Oil Refinery in Krasnodar Territory with drones, indicating cross‑regional strike capability (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Russian forces reported intense fighting in the Eastern Zaporozhye direction, with Ukrainian troops attempting to insert small groups to slow Russian advances (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Ukrainian air defenses reported engagements over the city of Zaporizhzhia, with multiple UAV attacks near Melitopol and damage to infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Ukrainian reports indicated a new group of Shahed UAVs heading north‑west over Zaporizhzhia, suggesting expanded UAV activity in the north‑west axis (Source: @kpszsu/64839).
A new UAV group was observed over northern Sumy heading toward Chernihiv direction, indicating potential threats to the northern supply lines (Source: @kpszsu/64832).
Ukrainian forces reported a Shahed strike on the city of Konotop in Sumy region, causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81837).
Ukrainian air defense claimed destruction of a Shahed over Poltava, reinforcing defensive posture in the central‑northern corridor (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67553).
Russian control over Okrimovka in Kharkiv region was announced, marking a shift in the northern front's territorial balance (Source: @tass_agency/380221).

Ukrainian forces destroyed four bridges on the Crimean approach, including two over the North Crimean Canal and the road bridge between Perekop and Armyansk, disrupting Russian logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/81836).
Ukrainian drones attacked the Chonhar Bridge on the Crimea‑Kherson border, leading to reversible traffic patterns and indicating pressure on southern supply routes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
A Shahed strike hit the Afipsky Oil Refinery in Krasnodar Territory, causing casualties and highlighting vulnerability of southern energy assets (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Ukrainian reports noted drone attacks on the Sevastopol fuel situation, with fuel tankers failing to arrive, affecting Russian naval operations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).
Russian forces reported ongoing attacks on the Mariupol port infrastructure, though Ukrainian sources claimed successful destruction of the port (Source: @UkraineNow/81834).
Ukrainian air defenses reported shooting down 195 hostile UAVs of various types across the country, demonstrating high‑intensity air defense activity (Source: @kpszsu/64833).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided bomb strikes on Donetsk region, indicating continued Russian air offensive capability (Source: @kpszsu/64843).
Ukrainian forces reported several UAV interceptions near Moscow, suggesting extended reach of Ukrainian drone operations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).

Ukrainian reports noted multiple Shahed attacks over Poltava, Sumy and Konotop, with some UAVs being reactive types, highlighting evolving UAV threat profiles (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67555; @mon1tor_ua/67542).
Russian air defenses engaged Ukrainian drones over the Bryansk and Belgorod regions, causing civilian injuries, showing cross‑border air engagements (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30836).

key_developments: Ukrainian air defenses claimed to have destroyed or suppressed 195 hostile Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and other UAVs across northern, southern and eastern Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/64833).
Ukrainian strikes damaged four bridges at the Crimean entrance, including two over the North Crimean Canal and the Perekop‑Armyansk road bridge (Source: @UkraineNow/81836).
Russian Ministry of Defense announced the liberation of Roskoshnoye in the DPR, marking a territorial gain (Source: @tass_agency/380222).
Russian forces reported establishing control over Okrimovka in Kharkiv region, expanding their foothold in the north‑east (Source: @tass_agency/380221).
A Shahed drone attack forced the Kuibyshev oil refinery to suspend operations, highlighting attacks on energy infrastructure (Source: @eRadarrua/96161).
Multiple reactive Shahed attacks over Poltava, Sumy and Konotop caused civilian casualties and infrastructure damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81837).
UN officials warned that May was the deadliest month for Ukrainian civilians since 2022, citing mass casualties in Sumy region (Source: @UkraineNow/81823).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian forces are intensifying UAV and precision strike campaigns, targeting Russian logistics, energy assets and bridge infrastructure, while Russian forces are consolidating gains in the Donetsk and Kharkiv regions. The high volume of hostile UAVs indicates a sustained aerial threat, prompting robust Ukrainian air defense responses. Civilian vulnerability remains acute, especially in contested northern and southern locales, raising the risk of broader humanitarian impact.
sections.regional_response: International actors are monitoring the escalation, with UN officials highlighting civilian casualties and diplomatic statements from Italy and Russia reflecting broader geopolitical concerns. Ukrainian requests for additional air defense systems suggest reliance on external support to counter the UAV surge. Russian diplomatic outreach, such as Lavrov's talks with Bahrain, indicates attempts to mitigate isolation amid intensified combat.
sections.intelligence_notes: The concentration of Shahed and reactive UAV attacks over key transport corridors and energy facilities suggests a coordinated effort to disrupt supply lines. Russian territorial announcements may be aimed at shaping narrative and morale. Monitoring of bridge damage and repair capabilities will be critical for assessing future Russian logistical resilience. Continued observation of UAV flight paths can inform predictive models for upcoming strike zones.
